# OCaml

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by .github/scripts/gen_language_docs.py - DO NOT EDIT.
     Edit the source matrix JSON / workflow and re-run the generator instead. -->

How `json2vars-setter` handles OCaml, and how the example workflow provisions it.

## json2vars-setter integration

| Item | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Matrix JSON key | `versions.ocaml` |
| Action output | `versions_ocaml` |
| Dynamic-update strategy input | `ocaml-strategy` (`stable` / `latest` / `both`) |
| Version source | see [Version Sources](../reference/version-sources.md) |

## Supported runners

The example matrix runs on **Ubuntu / macOS**:

`ubuntu-latest`, `macos-latest`

Any GitHub-hosted runner the setup action supports also works - the OS list is just the `os` array in your matrix JSON.

## Supported versions

Example matrix (what the example workflow runs):

```json
"versions": { "ocaml": ["5.2.1", "5.3.0"] }
```

> Enable dynamic update (`ocaml-strategy`) to have json2vars-setter fill these in from the latest releases. The `stable` / `latest` values for this language are populated into the version cache by the **Refresh data files** workflow.

## Setup action (example workflow)

[`ocaml_test.yml`](https://github.com/7rikazhexde/json2vars-setter/blob/main/.github/workflows/ocaml_test.yml) provisions OCaml with the matrix value json2vars-setter produced:

```yaml
- name: Set up OCaml
  uses: ocaml/setup-ocaml@v3.6.1
  with:
    ocaml-compiler: ${{ matrix.ocaml-version }}
```
